content: Most AI agent building guidance treats custom agent development as writing monolithic, integrated code from scratch, but the OpenClaw Agent Client Protocol (ACP) framework reveals that we can reframe agent building as assembling interchangeable, pluggable external harness components rather than building a single unified system. This means teams can mix and match specialized reasoning engines (from OpenAI GPT to Claude Code to custom local models) as interchangeable 'power modules' for an agent, rather than locking the entire agent to a single base LLM.
source_topics: agent harness components ACP openclaw autonomous agent framework
